rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Or Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Denied. Given that Petitioner named Jean Coblentz and Andrew Coblentz, as Co-Trustees of the Coblentz 1990 Revocable Trust, as successors in interest to William Coblentz in the 2012 Petition for Relief from Breach of Trust ("2012 Petition"), and that in 2014 he substituted the special administrator to be appointed to administer the estate of William Coblentz for Doe No. 1 in the 2012 Petition, the amendment in 2015 specifying as the correct name for Mr. Coblentz' post-death successor to be the "Estate of William Coblentz (deceased)" relates back to the filing of the 2012 Petition. Respondent has not established that the statute of limitations had necessarily run by September of 2012. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, February 22, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Respondent Kevin Mcclatchys Sixth, Twelfth, Thirteenth, Fourteenth And Fifteenth Affirmative Defenses
rulings.Rulings: Denied. Petitioner's moving papers do not establish that the any of the various statutes of limitation raised in the challenged affirmative defenses can have no application to any of the Petitioner's claims. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, February 22, 2017.
